posts.title: Technical Submission on India’s Digital Trade Facilitation Bill, 2026
posts.content:
Introduction

A sizeable portion of the Indian and global tech ecosystem was gathered at the India AI Impact Summit in New Delhi last week. While I missed attending it due to personal reasons, as an open source maintainer and an alumnus of The Takshashila Institution's GCPP

posts.title: RISC-ing It: An incoming landscape shift?
posts.content: The implications of China's RISC-V strategy on global semiconductor and open source ecosystems.
posts.contentSnippet: The implications of China's RISC-V strategy on global semiconductor and open source ecosystems.

posts.title: Open Source Security: The Good, The Bad, The Vulnerable
posts.content: Open source is the backbone of nearly every application in every industry! As great as this may seem for the movement, it raises important questions about our progress. This panel discussion at the State of Open Conference 2025 explores some of these q's and discusses future implications.
posts.contentSnippet: Open source is the backbone of nearly every application in every industry! As great as this may seem for the movement, it raises important questions about our progress. This panel discussion at the State of Open Conference 2025 explores some of these q's and discusses future implications.

posts.title: Behind the scenes of organising a community-driven event - part 2
posts.content: In the first part of the series, I discussed our why behind organizing a community-driven event that was also the very first edition of Kubernetes Community Days in Mumbai. In this post, we'll dive into the juicy bits of how we went about setting the stage (very literally)

posts.title: Behind the scenes of organising a community-driven event - part 1
posts.content:

posts.title: The complicated road to Open Source Sustainability
posts.content: Overworked maintainers & understaffed projects are the foundations that open source and, thereby, most of your daily drivers are built on. Corporate open source customers need to be better citizens in order to move towards a more sustainable model of open source.
posts.contentSnippet: Overworked maintainers & understaffed projects are the foundations that open source and, thereby, most of your daily drivers are built on. Corporate open source customers need to be better citizens in order to move towards a more sustainable model of open source.
